items.prompt: Who cast down his staff before Pharaoh?
items.explanation: Exodus 7 says Aaron cast down his staff before Pharaoh.

items.prompt: What did Pharaoh's magicians do after Aaron's staff became a serpent?

options: They did a similar thing with their staffs
They asked Moses to teach them
They pleaded for the Nile to be healed

correct_answer.value: They did a similar thing with their staffs

items.explanation: Exodus 7 says the magicians also cast down their staffs.

items.prompt: What happened to the magicians' staffs in Exodus 7?

options: They were thrown into the Nile

items.explanation: Exodus 7 says Aaron's staff swallowed the others.

items.prompt: What came up and covered the land of Egypt in Exodus 8?
items.explanation: Exodus 8 says frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.

items.prompt: What land was not struck by the hail in Exodus 9?
items.explanation: Exodus 9 says hail did not fall in the land of Goshen.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Exodus 7.

content.text: The water in the Nile turned to ____.

items.explanation: Exodus 7 says the water in the Nile turned to blood.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Exodus 8.

content.text: Pharaoh asked Moses to plead with the ____ to take away the frogs.

items.explanation: Exodus 8 says Pharaoh asked Moses to plead with the LORD about the frogs.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Exodus 8.

content.text: When Aaron struck the dust, it became ____ throughout Egypt.

items.explanation: Exodus 8 says the dust became gnats or lice throughout the land.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Exodus 9.

content.text: Pharaoh checked and found that not one of Israel's ____ had died.

items.explanation: Exodus 9 says Pharaoh found that not one of Israel's livestock had died.

items.prompt: Which image best represents the Nile water turning red in Exodus 7?

options.label: The Nile River turning red along an ancient Egyptian riverbank
options.alt: The Nile River turning red along an ancient Egyptian riverbank with reeds nearby

options.label: A staff transformed into a serpent on the floor before Pharaoh
options.alt: A staff transformed into a serpent on the floor before Pharaoh in an Egyptian court

options.label: Many frogs across an ancient Egyptian courtyard and riverbank
options.alt: Many frogs across an ancient Egyptian courtyard and riverbank

options.label: Hail falling over Egyptian fields under a stormy sky
options.alt: Hail falling over Egyptian fields under a stormy sky

correct_answer.value: The Nile River turning red along an ancient Egyptian riverbank

items.explanation: Exodus 7 describes the Nile water turning to blood.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Which image best represents the land of Goshen being set apart and protected?

options.label: Dust rising from the ground with gnats or lice suggested as a haze
options.alt: Dust rising from the ground in ancient Egypt with gnats or lice suggested as a haze

options.label: Swarms of flies over an ancient Egyptian setting
options.alt: Swarms of flies over an ancient Egyptian setting

options.label: A calm ancient Egyptian livestock field with herds grazing
options.alt: A calm ancient Egyptian livestock field with herds grazing

options.label: A peaceful protected land scene representing Goshen with calm light
options.alt: A peaceful protected land scene representing Goshen with tents, livestock, and calm light

correct_answer.value: A peaceful protected land scene representing Goshen with calm light

items.explanation: Exodus 8 and 9 describe Goshen as set apart from the plagues.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Put these Exodus 7 events in the order they appear.

items: Aaron's staff swallows the other staffs
Aaron casts down his staff before Pharaoh
The waters of Egypt turn to blood
Pharaoh's magicians also cast down their staffs

value: Aaron casts down his staff before Pharaoh
Pharaoh's magicians also cast down their staffs
Aaron's staff swallows the other staffs
The waters of Egypt turn to blood

items.explanation: Exodus 7 moves from the staff sign in Pharaoh's court to the Nile turning to blood.

items.prompt: Put these Exodus 8 plague events in the order they appear.

items: Pharaoh asks Moses to plead about the frogs

value: Pharaoh asks Moses to plead about the frogs

items.explanation: Exodus 8 records frogs first, then the plea about frogs, then gnats or lice, and then flies.

items.prompt: Put these Exodus 9 events in the order they appear.

items: Boils come on people and animals
A plague falls on the livestock of Egypt

value: A plague falls on the livestock of Egypt
Boils come on people and animals

items.explanation: Exodus 9 moves from livestock to boils, then the hail warning, and then Pharaoh's response after hail.

items.prompt: Match each Exodus 7 item with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: River struck so its water turned to blood
Also did something similar with their staffs
Object that swallowed the other staffs
Died when the river was struck

value.right: Object that swallowed the other staffs
River struck so its water turned to blood
Died when the river was struck
Also did something similar with their staffs

items.explanation: Exodus 7 connects Aaron's staff, the Nile, the fish, and the magicians with these details.

items.prompt: Match each Exodus 8 item with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: Place set apart from the swarm
Filled the houses and land later in the chapter

value.right: Filled the houses and land later in the chapter
Place set apart from the swarm

items.explanation: Exodus 8 ties frogs, dust, flies, and Goshen to these facts.

items.prompt: Match each Exodus 9 item with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: Ruler who checked on Israel's livestock
Plague that fell under a stormy sky
Plague that came from ashes thrown upward
Animals mentioned in the field plague

value.right: Animals mentioned in the field plague
Plague that came from ashes thrown upward
Plague that fell under a stormy sky
Ruler who checked on Israel's livestock

items.explanation: Exodus 9 links livestock, boils, hail, and Pharaoh's inquiry with these details.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Exodus 7.

content.statement: Exodus 7 says the magicians of Egypt did the same by their secret arts.

items.explanation: Exodus 7 says the magicians did the same by their secret arts.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Exodus 9.

content.statement: Exodus 9 says Pharaoh checked and found that not one of Israel's livestock had died.

items.explanation: Exodus 9 says Pharaoh checked and found Israel's livestock unharmed.

items.prompt: Find the factual mistake in this Exodus 8 statement.

content.statement: During the plague of flies, the land of Goshen was struck just like the rest of Egypt.
content.instruction: Spot the error in this statement.
options: During the plague of flies, Goshen was set apart and not struck like the rest of Egypt.
During the plague of flies, Moses stayed in Goshen and never spoke to Pharaoh.
During the plague of flies, the Nile alone was struck and no houses were affected.
During the plague of flies, Aaron gathered hail in the fields of Goshen.

correct_answer.value: During the plague of flies, Goshen was set apart and not struck like the rest of Egypt.

items.explanation: Exodus 8 says Goshen was set apart when the flies came on Egypt.
items.teaching_note: This keeps the focus on the distinction stated in the passage.
